chosen_title: Employer Didn't Deposit TDS — She Got the Demand
caption:
Your employer deducted TDS from your salary. But did they actually send it to the government?

In a real ITAT Mumbai case, an IT professional had ₹3.91 lakh deducted as TDS. Her employer deposited only a fraction — so just ₹79,000 showed up in her Form 26AS. The Income Tax Department sent her a ₹3.36 lakh demand. Zero fault of hers.

ITAT Mumbai ruled in her favour: full TDS credit must be granted when the employee can prove deduction through salary slips and Form 16 — even if the employer failed to deposit. But she had to fight all the way to a tribunal to get there.

Most salaried Indians never cross-check Form 26AS before filing. That is exactly how innocent taxpayers end up with shock notices.

What to do right now: Log in to incometax.gov.in → download your AIS and Form 26AS → match every TDS row against your Form 16 or salary slips → if there is a gap, raise a grievance on the portal before you file your ITR.

Spotted a mismatch? WhatsApp us — we will tell you exactly what to do next.

Note: general information, not professional advice — talk to us for your specific case.
Source: ITAT Mumbai, as reported on TaxGuru (2025)
#IncomeTax #TDS #Form26AS #ITR #ITRFiling #TaxFiling #PersonalFinanceIndia #CharteredAccountant #TaxPlanning #Vadodara #SalariedIndia #TaxSavingTips

slides.label: TAX FILES
slides.image_prompt: A young Indian woman in formal office attire staring at a printed government tax demand notice under a single desk lamp in a dark room, her hand pressed flat on the paper, expression frozen in disbelief, deep shadows, one harsh beam of light on the document
slides.headline: She owed nothing. Got a ₹3.36 lakh demand anyway.

slides.label: THE NUMBERS
slides.body: Deducted from her salary as TDS. Her employer deposited only ₹79,000 with the government. The tax department came after HER for the ₹3.36 lakh shortfall.

slides.label: REAL TALK
slides.headline: Your employer's failure can become your tax demand.

slides.label: MYTH vs FACT
slides.myth: If TDS is deducted from your salary, it is automatically deposited with the government.
slides.truth: Not always. Employers can deduct TDS from your pay but delay or skip the deposit entirely. Form 26AS only shows what was actually paid — and the gap quietly becomes your problem at filing time.

slides.label: TAX FILES
slides.title: Check this BEFORE you file your ITR
points: Match every TDS row against your Form 16 / salary slips
Gap found? Raise a grievance on the portal first
Keep all salary slips — they are your proof if it goes to tribunal
File only after TDS credits match your documents

slides.label: TAX FILES
slides.image_prompt: Close-up of two documents laid side by side on a wooden desk in warm morning light — a printed salary slip and a Form 26AS printout — with a red pen circling a highlighted figure on the Form 26AS, no faces, clean and documentary feel
slides.headline: ITAT ruled in her favour — but she had to prove it.
slides.caption: Salary slips were her evidence. Yours could be too.
